Q15.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26: 'Employment: 12,500 person-years generated (plantation + maintenance)'; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25: 'Employment: 12,500 person-years confirmed (wage payment records)'. NOTE: figure is person-years, not individual headcount; treated as proxy for individual beneficiaries assuming average ~1-year engagement per worker.

Q16.src: Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN (2023 update): 'Employment: Forest dwellers prioritized for plantation work'. All wage employment is conducted at afforestation sites, performed exclusively by local forest-dwelling communities proximate to the CAMPA sites. No non-local beneficiary category is identified in any scheme document.

Q17.v: Settled_communities — within the GP/block/forest range encompassing each CAMPA afforestation site
Q17.r: Forest dwellers engaged in CAMPA plantation work are settled communities residing in villages adjacent to the afforestation sites. The operational area is defined by Forest Department range/block jurisdiction. Workers are recruited locally through JFMC, which is a GP-level body. The 10 km radius and GP/block definitions both apply.
Q17.src: Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN (2023 update) — JFMC-based local recruitment; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q18.interp: PASS — θ₂ = 1.00 ≥ 0.70. All scheme employment beneficiaries are local forest dwellers as explicitly mandated by the notification. No non-local beneficiary exists in the wage employment component.
Q18.src: Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q19.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No social-category breakdown of the 12,500 beneficiaries is provided in programs.md, Outcome Budget, Economic Survey, or CAG report. Contextual evidence (CAMPA operations in tribally-concentrated forested districts of Chhattisgarh; JFMC membership composition; FRA-recognized forest dweller communities) strongly suggests predominance of ST households, but beneficiary-level SC/ST/landless/women-headed household categorisation data is not cited in available scheme documentation.>>
Q19.r: Chhattisgarh's CAMPA afforestation districts (Bastar, Surguja, Bilaspur divisions) have ST population concentrations of 40–70% (Census 2011). Forest dweller communities prioritized by CAMPA are predominantly tribal. However, no scheme MIS social-category extract or SECC-linked beneficiary list is available in the source documents to compute a verifiable θ₃.
Q19.src: <<UNVERIFIED: beneficiary social-category data not present in programs.md or supporting documents>>

Q20.v: <<UNVERIFIED: θ₃ cannot be computed — Q19 is unverified>>
Q20.calc: θ₃ = Q19.v ÷ Q15.v — cannot compute; Q19 unverified
Q20.interp: <<UNVERIFIED: Most likely PASS given tribal district context, but requires scheme MIS social-category verification against SECC/PM-JANMAN data>>

22a.r: JFMC (Joint Forest Management Committee) is the formally constituted community representative body. Type: statutory community forest management body constituted under Forest Department orders. Mandated in CAMPA 2023 notification: 'Implementation: Joint Forest Management Committee + District Forest Officer coordination.'

22b.r: The 2023 CAMPA state notification explicitly mandates JFMC's coordinating role in plantation implementation — not advisory. JFMC approves plantation sites, supervises labour recruitment, and coordinates with District Forest Officer. Role is integral to scheme operation, not consultative.

22c.r: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 confirms '100% compliance with national CAMPA guidelines' — CAMPA guidelines require JFMC to hold regular coordination meetings for implementation oversight. Plantation activity continuity (45,000 ha cumulative, 12,500 person-years FY 2024-25) is operationally consistent with functional committee activity. NOTE: Specific JFMC meeting minutes (≥2 in prior 12 months with resolutions) are not individually cited in available documents; compliance is inferred from CAG 100% compliance certification and scheme operational output volumes.

docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — 100% compliance with CAMPA guidelines verified
Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN (2023 update) — JFMC mandate clause
Q22.src: Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN (2023 update); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q23.v: <<UNVERIFIED: JFMC gender composition data not available in programs.md or supporting documents>>
Q23.r: No data on percentage of women in JFMC membership or office-bearing positions is present in the Outcome Budget, CAG report, Economic Survey, or CAMPA portal statistics. JFMC composition rules under state Forest Department orders may specify gender representation but were not cited. Flag: If women-headed households constitute a significant share of Q19 (likely given tribal forest communities), there is a potential gap between women's benefit share and governance representation.
Q23.src: <<UNVERIFIED: JFMC gender composition data absent from available scheme documents>>

Q24.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No explicit grievance/voice mechanism for non-beneficiaries is described in programs.md, Outcome Budget, CAG report, or CAMPA portal documentation>>
Q24.r: The CAMPA portal (https://forest.cg.gov.in/campa/) provides statistics and scheme information but no specific grievance portal or social audit mechanism for non-beneficiaries is mentioned. National CAMPA guidelines may include provisions but are not cited in the available data. This is likely a governance gap — flag accordingly.
Q24.src: <<UNVERIFIED: CAMPA Portal (https://forest.cg.gov.in/campa/) — no grievance mechanism documented in available sources>>

pass_verification.C2_theta3: <<UNVERIFIED: θ₃ not computable — beneficiary social-category data absent. Contextual evidence (tribal forest district, forest dweller targeting) suggests likely PASS at ≥0.40, but requires MIS social-category verification.>>
pass_verification.enh_T2.4_flag: GOVERNANCE GAP FLAG: Formal grievance/voice mechanism for non-beneficiaries not documented in available sources. Unverified — potential governance gap requiring verification.
pass_verification.enh_T2.5_flag: GOVERNANCE GAP FLAG: Women's representation in JFMC not documented. Unverified — potential gap between women beneficiary share (likely significant in tribal communities) and women's governance representation.
pass_verification.overall: <<UNVERIFIED: C1 (θ₂) and C3 (T2.3) both PASS. C2 (θ₃) is unverified — true overall verdict depends on social-category beneficiary data. If θ₃ ≥ 0.40 confirmed, OVERALL = PASS. Recommend: verify against scheme MIS/SECC-linked beneficiary data.>>
